jobs.title: Agricultural Water Quality Specialist
jobs.description:
**Contact**

Sarah Lopez, Executive Director

office@ccwqp.org

**About the Company**

Central Coast Water Quality Preservation, Inc. (or “Preservation, Inc.” as we are known locally) is a farmer\-directed nonprofit that assists Central Coast farmers in complying with regulations related to the Clean Water Act. We work with farmers, regulators, and other stakeholders in the community to bridge the gap between clean water and healthy food. We implement scientifically rigorous programs that are also cost\-effective for our grower members. Preservation, Inc’s small office environment can include 2 to 5 additional staff depending on the time of year. Visit our website to learn more: ccwqp.org

**Position Summary**

The Agricultural Water Quality Specialist supports a variety of technical and outreach needs including data management and analysis; field visits to monitoring sites and member farms; and communicating water quality monitoring and fertilizer reporting results to the agricultural community as well as environmental and regulatory stakeholders.

This position serves as the lead role in surface water programs, which includes a newly developed follow\-up program. In addition, there is potential for the position to be involved in our groundwater monitoring program. The work requires strong analytical and data management skills, as well as the ability to communicate science and regulatory compliance to diverse audiences.

The Agricultural Water Quality Staff also interacts frequently (in\-person and virtually) with a large network of colleagues, contractors, partner organizations, and member farmers. Same\-day travel to nearby locations is generally required on a weekly or monthly basis. Overnight travel to more distant locations (3 to 5 hours drive) can be needed a few times per year, as our organization serves farms from northern Santa Cruz County to as far south as Santa Barbara.

**KEY RESPONSIBIITIES:**

* Lead surface water monitoring and follow\-up programs.
* Conduct field visits to monitoring sites and member farms.
* Analyze and manage environmental monitoring data and fertilizer reporting information.
* Prepare reports, presentations, and communications for growers, regulators, and stakeholders.
* Communicate technical and regulatory information to agricultural producers and community partners.
* Coordinate with laboratories, consultants, contractors, and partner organizations.
* Support groundwater monitoring activities as needed.
* Identify opportunities for program improvements and operational efficiencies.

**QUALIFICATIONS:**

Required Education and Experience:

* Bachelor's degree in Agronomy, Soil Science, Environmental Science, Marine Science, Geology, Hydrogeology, Hydrology, Chemistry, or a related field.
* 3\-5\+ years of related work experience in an academic, agricultural, or environmental setting.
* Experience working with scientific, environmental, agricultural, or compliance\-related data sets.
* Experience interpreting and communicating technical information to a variety of audiences, including agricultural producers, regulatory agencies, and community stakeholders.

Technical Skills:

* Proficiency with MS Excel, MS Word, and MS PowerPoint.
* Strong data management, analysis, and quality control skills.
* Ability to identify trends, interpret findings, and prepare reports or presentations based on technical data.

Role Requirements:

* Valid driver’s license
* Ability to travel to field/ranch sites located within a 15 minute to 2 hour drive approximately 4 times per month
* Ability to travel overnight to field/ranch sites located within a 3 to 5 hour drive approximately 4\-6 times per year
* Ability to manage multiple projects and competing deadlines while maintaining a high level of accuracy and attention to detail.
* Ability to collaborate effectively with farmers, contractors, regulatory agencies, partner organizations, and internal team members.
* Strong verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to translate technical and regulatory information for non\-technical audiences.

Preferred Qualifications:

* Master’s degree or PhD in a related field.
* Bilingual English/Spanish proficiency.
* Experience with agricultural water quality monitoring, nutrient management, groundwater management, or regulatory compliance programs.
* Experience using ArcGIS, R or Python, MS Access, statistical software, CropManage, MODFLOW, SWAT, or similar analytical tools.

**WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:**

* Serves as the primary staff lead for surface water monitoring programs and associated follow\-up activities.
* Maintains accurate, organized, and reliable environmental and program data to support regulatory compliance and member reporting obligations.
* Builds productive working relationships with growers, regulators, consultants, laboratories, and partner organizations.
* Communicates technical findings and regulatory requirements in a clear, practical, and actionable manner for member growers.
* Successfully manages field activities, reporting deadlines, and stakeholder communications with a high degree of ownership and accountability.
* Identifies opportunities to improve program efficiency, data quality, and member support services as regulations and program needs evolve.

**JOB TYPE/DETAILS:**

* Full\-Time
* The estimated hiring range is $38\.00\-$58\.00/hour. Pay dependent on experience and skills.
* Location: Salinas, California. Candidates in the Santa Maria/San Luis Obispo areas may be eligible for additional remote work flexibility based on business needs and field coverage requirements.
* Eligible for Paid Time Off (PTO), health and dental insurance, 401k, and annual bonus opportunities.
* Eligible for a 1 day/week Work\-From\-Home arrangement (exceptions may be made for staff based in San Luis Obispo, Santa Maria, or other approved remote locations).

**WHY JOIN PRESERVATION, INC?**

* Make a direct impact on agricultural sustainability and water

jobs.title: SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
jobs.description:
**body { font\-family:** "Source Sans Pro",sans\-serif; } caption { visibility: hidden; } /\*h3 { all: unset; }\*/ /\*p { all: unset; }\*/ .primaryPostingHeader { color: \#FDB81E; font\-size: 22pt; } .secondaryPostingHeader { font\-size: 20pt; color: \#02BFE7; } .postingHeader { font\-weight: 200; font\-size: 18pt; margin\-top: 15px; margin\-bottom: 15px; } .col { display: inline\-block; min\-height: 1px; } .col\-twothirds { width: 66%; } .col\-onethird { width: 33%; } .col\-half { width: 49%; } .paddingTop10 { padding\-top: 10px; } .jcHead { display: inline\-block; width: 100%; background\-color: \#323A45; color: white; } .jcHead\-RightPane { vertical\-align: top; min\-width: 150px; } .jcBand { padding\-top: 10px; padding\-bottom: 10px; padding\-left: 35px; padding\-right: 35px; } .jcBand: nth\-child(2n\+1\) { background\-color: whitesmoke; } .stateSealLogo { display: none; padding\-left: 50px; padding\-bottom: 20px; } @media only screen and (min\-width: 1300px) { .stateSealLogo { display: inline\-block; } } @media only screen and (max\-width: 1024px) { .col\-half { width: 100%; } .col\-onethird { width: 100%; } .col\-twothirds { width: 100%; } } .boldHeading { margin\-top: 15px; font\-weight: bold; } .wHeaderCol { width: 125px; }
  
  

**Job Posting:** Unit Chief
  
  

Department of Toxic Substances Control
  
  

JC\-523280 \- Unit Chief
  
SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
  
  

$10,854\.00 \- $13,493\.00 per Month
  
New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.
  
  

**Final Filing Date:** 7/15/2026
  
  

Job Description and Duties
  
  

Are you an experienced leader looking for a rewarding technical career opportunity? This position supervises a multidisciplinary team of scientists, engineers and geologists responsible for resolving complex landfill projects. If you enjoy partnering with technical leads to developing effective solutions to challenging issues, we encourage you to apply!
  
  

As a Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory) you will have the opportunity to:
  
  

Provide direction and training to staff who oversee environmental investigations and cleanup activities at properties where hazardous materials, substances or waste were released into the environment
  
Ensure that the requirements of legal mandates, which DTSC is party to, are implemented in an appropriate manner
  
Provide guidance on staff’s review of submitted site cleanup related documents (e.g., Phase I, Preliminary Endangerment Assessment/Resource Conservation and Recovery Act Facility Assessment, Engineering Evaluation/Cost Analysis, Site Characterization work plans and reports, Remedial Investigation/ Feasibility Studies, Sampling and Analysis Plans, Human Health and Ecological Risk Assessment, etc.), field work oversight, regulatory decision making, and communication with responsible parties, project proponents, external agencies, and the public
  
Monitor staff’s review of site cleanup\-related documents for compliance with applicable standards and requirements, conduct oversight of field work consistent with DTSC approved work plans, and ensure remediation work meets federal and state standards
  
Provide technical consultation and support to staff on most complex technical issues including investigations, data analysis, removal actions, remedial actions, and implementation of any repair, modification, or improvements to the site
  
Oversee and assist staff in evaluating appropriate sampling and mitigation techniques, based on state of practice scientific and engineering practices, or as appropriate, uses innovative technologies to address unique and/or complex problems
  
  

**Desirable Qualifications We Look For:**  

Strong leadership skills
  
Experience with CERCLA environmental cleanup process
  
Strong communication skills
  
Strong problem\-solving skills
  
Experience with large/complex projects
  
Experience with soil gas and groundwater remediation projects
  
  

Working Conditions
  
  

This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship. Applicants must be authorized to work in the United States without the need for visa sponsorship by the start date of employment. The Department of Toxic Substances Control does not participate in E\-Verify for employment authorization purposes.
  
  

A hybrid schedule may be available for this position.
  
  

DTSC \- Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB)
  
  

Position Details
  
**Job Code \#:** JC\-523280
  
  

**Position \#(s):**  

810\-591\-0764\-001
  
**Working Title:** Unit Chief
  
**Classification:** SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
  
$10,854\.00 \- $13,493\.00
  
New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.
  
  

**\# of Positions:** 1
  
**Work Location:** Sacramento County
  
**Telework:** Hybrid
  
**Job Type:** Permanent, Full Time
  
**Facility:** Cal Center Regional Office
  
  

Department Information
  
  

This position is located at DTSC’s Cal Center Regional office in Sacramento, CA.
  
  

The Department of Toxic Substances Control (DTSC) is a member of the boards, departments, and offices (BDOs) within the California Environmental Protection Agency (CalEPA).
  
  

**Our Mission:** To protect California’s people, communities, and environment from toxic substances, to enhance economic vitality by restoring contaminated land, and to compel manufacturers to make safer consumer products.
  
  

**Our Vision:** All of California is thriving in a healthy environment.
  
  

**Our Core Values:** DTSC values diversity, equity, and inclusion throughout the organization. We foster an environment where employees from a variety of backgrounds, cultures, and personal experiences are welcomed and can thrive. We believe the diversity of our employees is essential to inspiring innovative solutions. Together we further ou

jobs.title: Director of Risk Assessment
jobs.description:
**Position Summary**


The Director of Risk Assessment will report directly to the General Counsel and play a crucial role in managing and mitigating risks within our long\-term care facilities across California. This position requires a highly skilled and experienced professional with a strong background in nursing administration and a deep understanding of state regulations and standards.

**Essential Duties and Responsibilities:**

* Collaborate with the General Counsel to prepare legal analyses of clinical records.
* Conduct comprehensive risk assessments and develop strategies to mitigate potential risks.
* Ensure compliance with California Department of Public Health (CDPH) regulations and standards.
* Oversee and evaluate staffing standards to ensure optimal care and compliance with state requirements.
* Travel to various facilities throughout California to conduct on\-site assessments and provide support.
* Understand and apply workers' compensation standards and regulations to minimize liabilities.
* Provide training and guidance to staff on risk management practices and regulatory compliance.

**Qualifications**

* Registered Nurse (RN) or Licensed Nurse (LN) credentials.
* Minimum of 5 years of experience in the long\-term care industry.
* Proven experience in nursing administration within California.
* In\-depth knowledge of staffing standards and regulations in California.
* Ability to assist General Counsel with legal analysis of clinical records.
* Willingness to travel extensively within California.
* Thorough understanding of workers' compensation standards and CDPH regulations.

**Skills and Competencies**

* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
* Detail\-oriented with a high level of accuracy.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
* Proficient in using relevant software and tools for risk assessment and compliance tracking.

 **Working Conditions**

* May encounter frequent interruptions.
* May be involved with residents, family, and government agencies.
* May be requested to work beyond scheduled working hours at times.
* Is subject to call\-back during emergency conditions (e.g., severe weather, evacuation, post\-disaster, etc.)
* May be subjected to odors, dust, disinfectants, tobacco smoking or air contaminants.
* May be exposed to infectious waste, diseases, conditions, etc., including TB and the AIDS and Hepatitis B
* viruses.
* May be exposed to or handle hazardous chemicals.

 **Physical Requirements**

* Must be able to move intermittently throughout the day.
* Working throughout the nursing areas
* Must be able to lift up to 25 pounds, lifting more weight up to 100 pounds requires assistance.
* Ability to read fine print on tablet, progress notes and/or medical labels.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist/Fish Biologist
jobs.description:
Environmental Scientist/Fish Biologist

**Introduction:**

Point Environmental LLC is a small consulting firm based in Oregon City which helps its clients maintain compliance with environmental regulations in the construction and resource management industries throughout Oregon and Washington. The company is looking for a full\-time **Environmental Scientist/Fish Biologist** (Bio) to serve primarily in our southern service area. The southern service area extends from Oregon City to Eugene, and the company would prefer to hire an individual who currently resides between Oregon City and Salem.

The Bio will be assigned equipment, a company vehicle, and will be expected to report to jobsites or the Oregon City office directly from their residence the majority of the time. Therefore, their residence should be in the southern service area but within comfortable commuting distance of the Oregon City office at 914 Molalla Ave, Oregon City, OR 97045\.

The Bio’s primary responsibilities will involve providing environmental permit compliance and construction site erosion and sediment control inspection. The Bio’s other duties will include various environmental compliance tasks, particularly assisting with fish rescue/salvage operations during the summer in\-water work period, ESA species compliance, CWA compliance, and NEPA compliance.

The Bio is expected to work normal hours from 8am to 5pm Monday through Friday, but within that time, will be largely managing their own schedule which varies depending on weather conditions and project activities. Therefore, the individual needs to be self\-motivated and highly dependable.

**Responsibilities include:**

· Travel to multiple sites in Oregon, Washington, and occasionally Idaho

· Construction site inspection

· Fish salvage

· Remote stream survey

· Log entries using written and digital formats

· Thorough photo documentation

· Record keeping

· Report writing

· Communication with superintendents, foreman, and clients

· Spending long periods of time on your feet walking in inclement weather and rough terrain

**Job Requirements:**

· Bachelor's degree in fish biology, environmental science, or similar field

· At least **one (1\) of the** following Oregon DEQ acceptable erosion and sediment control inspection certifications:

o Certified Professional in Erosion and Sediment Control (CPESC)

o Certified Professional in Storm Water Quality (CPSWQ)

o Certified Erosion and Sediment Control Lead (CESCL)

o Rogue Valley Sewer Services Erosion and Sediment Control Certification

· Minimum 1 year experience (Please Include a List of Projects)

· Valid driver’s license

· Need to provide own weather appropriate clothing including above the ankle leather work boots. Point Environmental will provide the necessary safety PPE.

· Must currently have reliable transportation.

· A motivated and organized individual with honesty, integrity, who wants to work, and who is willing to learn and share their individuality and ideas to better the company.

· Construction casual attire and appearance, e.g. jeans or kakis with collard shirt or Point Environmental T\-shirt.

· Experience with Microsoft Office Suite and Adobe Acrobat Pro

**Job Preferences:**

· Preference will be given to those individuals with an educational emphasis and additional work experience in fish biology, electrofishing, forestry, civil construction, QGIS, etc.

**Compensation:**

The Bio will be an “at will” employee who will receive an hourly compensation commensurate with experience.

**Hiring Schedule:**

Immediately. Interviews will be scheduled with acceptable candidates.

**Benefits:**

Health/Dental insurance is available for employees working 30 hours a week or more.

Paid time off

Remote work

Company vehicle or paid mileage

Career training

Self\-management, flexible

Small company, relaxed atmosphere

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $25\.00 \- $30\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Environmental Health Officer
jobs.description:
Organisation Unit:
Location:
Tenure:
Closing:
Synopsis:
At the City of Melville, we're committed to creating a safe, healthy and sustainable environment for our residents. We're looking for motivated Environmental Health Officer to join our dedicated team and play a vital role in protecting public health through education, compliance and proactive environmental health initiatives.


Working within our Environmental Health team, you'll undertake a diverse range of regulatory, advisory and educational activities while partnering with residents, businesses and internal stakeholders to deliver high\-quality environmental health outcomes.


If you enjoy working collaboratively, solving complex problems and engaging with the community, we'd love to hear from you.


**Location:** City of Melville Civic Centre


**Salary:** $83,200\.00 to $124,488\.00 pro\-rata p.a. \+ 12% Super


**Employment Type:** Permanent, Full Time

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**


* Conducting inspections, assessments and investigations across a variety of environmental health matters
* Assessing development applications and processing environmental health applications
* Delivering proactive environmental health programs, sampling and compliance activities
* Providing professional advice to residents, businesses and internal stakeholders
* Supporting enforcement activities, including investigations and legal proceedings where required
* Mentoring the Environmental Health Assistant and contributing to continuous improvement across the team
* Assisting the Senior Environmental Health Officer in delivering efficient and effective environmental health services
  

**What We’re Looking For**


* A current ‘C’ Class Driver's Licence
* Qualifications recognised for appointment as an Environmental Health Officer in Western Australia
* Eligibility for appointment as an Authorised Person and Inspector under relevant legislation
* Demonstrated experience in environmental health inspections, investigations and compliance activities
* Sound knowledge of relevant environmental health legislation, including the Food Act 2008, Health (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1911 and Environmental Protection Act 1986
* Strong communication, negotiation and report writing skills
* The ability to manage competing priorities while delivering high\-quality customer service
* Local Government experience, food safety auditing knowledge and previous mentoring experience will be highly regarded
  

**How to Apply**


Apply online via the Apply button and provide a comprehensive resume and cover letter (no more than 2 pages) demonstrating fulfilment of essential requirements outlined in the Position Description attached.


**Closing Date: Sunday, 12 July 2026, 11\.59PM AWST.**


For further information about this position, please contact: Tony Nguyen, Acting Coordinator Environmental Health on 08 9364 0279\.


If you are interested in this exciting opportunity, we strongly advise you apply as soon as possible as the City reserves the right to close this advertisement before the closing date.

  

**Why The City of Melville?**


We are a welcoming and diverse community that enjoys a blend of retail and business precincts and an abundance of open spaces in a unique, natural landscape, along the shores of the beautiful Swan and Canning Rivers.


We are proud to be recognised as an Inclusive Employer 2024\-2025 – a prestigious award that benchmarks workplace inclusion across various diversity areas. This recognition is the only one of its kind in Australia and highlights our commitment to fostering a workplace where diversity is celebrated, and inclusion thrives. In everything we do, we focus on our EPIC values of Excellence, Participation, Integrity and Caring and we aim to achieve an inclusive, vibrant, and sustainable future.


**Why Belong?**


Elevate your work experience with the City of Melville’s benefits!


* Enjoy annual salary increases of 4%, effective each year from 1 July 2025 to 1 July 2027
* Enjoy flexible working arrangements that help balance work and life
* Stay active with a free LeisureFit membership
* Save time and money with free on\-site parking
* Celebrate your special day with Birthday Leave – no work on your big day!
* Access paid parental leave to support your family
* Take more time to recharge with 2 extra days of leave plus the option to purchase additional leave (up to 10 weeks off per year)
* Benefit from a generous superannuation co\-contribution of up to 15\.5%
* Maximise your pay with salary packaging and novated leasing options
* Grow your career with structured professional development and study assistance programs
* Look after your wellbeing with wellness initiatives and Employee Assistance Programs
* Connect outside of work with a vibrant social club
  

The City of Melville celebrates diversity, inclusion, and belonging. We welcome and strongly encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds, including but not limited to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people from culturally diverse backgrounds, and people living with disabilities.


All candidates applying for a position where the use of ‘plant and machinery’ or driving a vehicle is an inherent requirement of the role and other ‘high risk’ facilities where public safety is at risk, will be required to pass a drug and alcohol test as part of a pre\-employment medical.


We appreciate the interest from Recruitment Agencies in collaborating with us, however, at this time, the City of Melville is managing the recruitment process for this role. Please refrain from submitting candidate profiles to our team. Rest assured, should the need for external assistance arise in the future, we will not hesitate to reach out to recruitment agencies.


*This collection notice sets out how the City of Melville will manage your personal information. We will handle any personal information you provide in accordance with the standards set out in the Privacy and Respo

· Must currently have reliable transportation.

· A motivated and organized individual with honesty, integrity, who wants to work, and who is willing to learn and share their individuality and ideas to better the company.

· Construction casual attire and appearance, e.g. jeans or kakis with collard shirt or Point Environmental T\-shirt.

· Experience with Microsoft Office Suite and Adobe Acrobat Pro

**Job Preferences:**

· Preference will be given to those individuals with an educational emphasis and additional work experience in fish biology, electrofishing, forestry, civil construction, QGIS, etc.

**Compensation:**

The Bio will be an “at will” employee who will receive an hourly compensation commensurate with experience.

**Hiring Schedule:**

Immediately. Interviews will be scheduled with acceptable candidates.

**Benefits:**

Health/Dental insurance is available for employees working 30 hours a week or more.

Paid time off

Remote work

Company vehicle or paid mileage

Career training

Self\-management, flexible

Small company, relaxed atmosphere

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $25\.00 \- $30\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

new_jobs.title: Environmental Health Officer
new_jobs.description:
Organisation Unit:
Location:
Tenure:
Closing:
Synopsis:
At the City of Melville, we're committed to creating a safe, healthy and sustainable environment for our residents. We're looking for motivated Environmental Health Officer to join our dedicated team and play a vital role in protecting public health through education, compliance and proactive environmental health initiatives.


Working within our Environmental Health team, you'll undertake a diverse range of regulatory, advisory and educational activities while partnering with residents, businesses and internal stakeholders to deliver high\-quality environmental health outcomes.


If you enjoy working collaboratively, solving complex problems and engaging with the community, we'd love to hear from you.


**Location:** City of Melville Civic Centre


**Salary:** $83,200\.00 to $124,488\.00 pro\-rata p.a. \+ 12% Super


**Employment Type:** Permanent, Full Time

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**


* Conducting inspections, assessments and investigations across a variety of environmental health matters
* Assessing development applications and processing environmental health applications
* Delivering proactive environmental health programs, sampling and compliance activities
* Providing professional advice to residents, businesses and internal stakeholders
* Supporting enforcement activities, including investigations and legal proceedings where required
* Mentoring the Environmental Health Assistant and contributing to continuous improvement across the team
* Assisting the Senior Environmental Health Officer in delivering efficient and effective environmental health services
  

**What We’re Looking For**


* A current ‘C’ Class Driver's Licence
* Qualifications recognised for appointment as an Environmental Health Officer in Western Australia
* Eligibility for appointment as an Authorised Person and Inspector under relevant legislation
* Demonstrated experience in environmental health inspections, investigations and compliance activities
* Sound knowledge of relevant environmental health legislation, including the Food Act 2008, Health (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1911 and Environmental Protection Act 1986
* Strong communication, negotiation and report writing skills
* The ability to manage competing priorities while delivering high\-quality customer service
* Local Government experience, food safety auditing knowledge and previous mentoring experience will be highly regarded
  

**How to Apply**


Apply online via the Apply button and provide a comprehensive resume and cover letter (no more than 2 pages) demonstrating fulfilment of essential requirements outlined in the Position Description attached.


**Closing Date: Sunday, 12 July 2026, 11\.59PM AWST.**


For further information about this position, please contact: Tony Nguyen, Acting Coordinator Environmental Health on 08 9364 0279\.


If you are interested in this exciting opportunity, we strongly advise you apply as soon as possible as the City reserves the right to close this advertisement before the closing date.

  

**Why The City of Melville?**


We are a welcoming and diverse community that enjoys a blend of retail and business precincts and an abundance of open spaces in a unique, natural landscape, along the shores of the beautiful Swan and Canning Rivers.


We are proud to be recognised as an Inclusive Employer 2024\-2025 – a prestigious award that benchmarks workplace inclusion across various diversity areas. This recognition is the only one of its kind in Australia and highlights our commitment to fostering a workplace where diversity is celebrated, and inclusion thrives. In everything we do, we focus on our EPIC values of Excellence, Participation, Integrity and Caring and we aim to achieve an inclusive, vibrant, and sustainable future.


**Why Belong?**


Elevate your work experience with the City of Melville’s benefits!


* Enjoy annual salary increases of 4%, effective each year from 1 July 2025 to 1 July 2027
* Enjoy flexible working arrangements that help balance work and life
* Stay active with a free LeisureFit membership
* Save time and money with free on\-site parking
* Celebrate your special day with Birthday Leave – no work on your big day!
* Access paid parental leave to support your family
* Take more time to recharge with 2 extra days of leave plus the option to purchase additional leave (up to 10 weeks off per year)
* Benefit from a generous superannuation co\-contribution of up to 15\.5%
* Maximise your pay with salary packaging and novated leasing options
* Grow your career with structured professional development and study assistance programs
* Look after your wellbeing with wellness initiatives and Employee Assistance Programs
* Connect outside of work with a vibrant social club
  

The City of Melville celebrates diversity, inclusion, and belonging. We welcome and strongly encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds, including but not limited to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people from culturally diverse backgrounds, and people living with disabilities.


All candidates applying for a position where the use of ‘plant and machinery’ or driving a vehicle is an inherent requirement of the role and other ‘high risk’ facilities where public safety is at risk, will be required to pass a drug and alcohol test as part of a pre\-employment medical.


We appreciate the interest from Recruitment Agencies in collaborating with us, however, at this time, the City of Melville is managing the recruitment process for this role. Please refrain from submitting candidate profiles to our team. Rest assured, should the need for external assistance arise in the future, we will not hesitate to reach out to recruitment agencies.


*This collection notice sets out how the City of Melville will manage your personal information. We will handle any personal information you provide in accordance with the standards set out in the Privacy and Respo
